title: Algorithm to Check 2NF
description: Procedure to determine if a relation is in Second Normal Form
code:
PROCEDURE Check2NF(Relation R, Set FDs, PrimaryKey PK)
BEGIN
 // Check if relation is in 1NF
 IF NOT Is1NF(R) THEN
 RETURN "Not in 1NF"
 END IF

 // Check for partial dependencies
 FOR EACH FD in FDs WHERE FD.LHS is proper subset of PK DO
 // FD.LHS is part of primary key
 // Check if FD.RHS contains non-key attributes
 IF ContainsNonKeyAttributes(FD.RHS, R, PK) THEN
 RETURN "Violates 2NF: Partial dependency " + FD.LHS + " → " + FD.RHS
 END IF
 END FOR

 RETURN "In 2NF"
END
explanation: The algorithm first verifies 1NF compliance, then checks if any functional dependency has a left-hand side that is only part of the composite primary key. If such a partial dependency exists with non-key attributes on the right side, it violates 2NF.

title: Algorithm to Find Candidate Keys
description: Procedure to find all candidate keys using attribute closure
code:
PROCEDURE FindCandidateKeys(Attributes AttrSet, Set FDs)
BEGIN
 candidateKeys = empty set
 superkeys = FindAllSuperKeys(AttrSet, FDs)

 FOR EACH superkey SK in superkeys DO
 isMinimal = TRUE
 FOR EACH attribute A in SK DO
 // Check if removing A still gives superkey
 tempSK = SK - {A}
 IF Closure(tempSK, FDs) contains all attributes THEN
 isMinimal = FALSE
 BREAK
 END IF
 END FOR

 IF isMinimal THEN
 add SK to candidateKeys
 END IF
 END FOR

 RETURN candidateKeys
END

FUNCTION Closure(AttrSet, FDs)
BEGIN
 result = AttrSet
 changed = TRUE
 WHILE changed DO
 changed = FALSE
 FOR EACH FD X → Y in FDs DO
 IF X is subset of result AND Y is not subset of result THEN
 result = result ∪ Y
 changed = TRUE
 END IF
 END FOR
 END WHILE
 RETURN result
END
explanation: Uses attribute closure to find all superkeys, then filters to find minimal superkeys (candidate keys). An attribute set is a superkey if its closure equals all attributes.

title: BCNF Decomposition Algorithm
description: Algorithm to decompose relation into Boyce-Codd Normal Form
code:
PROCEDURE BCNFDecompose(Relation R, Set FDs)
BEGIN
 result = {R} // Start with original relation
 decomposed = FALSE

 WHILE NOT decomposed DO
 decomposed = TRUE

 FOR EACH relation S in result DO
 // Find BCNF violation: FD X → Y where X is not a superkey
 FOR EACH FD X → Y in FDs restricted to S DO
 IF NOT IsSuperKey(X, S, FDs) THEN
 // Violation found, decompose S
 S1 = Closure(X, FDs) // Attributes functionally determined by X
 S2 = Attributes(S) - (S1 - X) // Remaining attributes

 Remove S from result
 Add S1 to result
 Add S2 to result

 decomposed = FALSE
 BREAK all loops
 END IF
 END FOR
 IF NOT decomposed THEN BREAK END IF
 END FOR
 END WHILE

 RETURN result
END
explanation: Repeatedly finds BCNF violations (determinants that aren't superkeys) and decomposes the relation. S1 contains the functional dependency's closure plus the determinant, S2 contains remaining attributes.
